questions.question: Which standard of the Care Certificate is 'Your Personal Development'?
options: Standard 1
Standard 2
Standard 8
Standard 16

questions.question: What is 'feedback' as described in this course?
options: A once-a-year score of your overall performance
A list of tasks that fall outside your job description
A formal written warning from your manager
Information about how your work is seen by others

questions.question: If something goes wrong during a shift, what does the course recommend you do?
options: Wait until your next appraisal before mentioning it
Put it down to the person's needs changing and move on
Be honest about it, report it where required, and reflect on it
Keep it to yourself so that no one worries

questions.question: What does reflective practice do with your everyday work?
options: It is only useful after something has gone wrong
It turns everyday experience into learning
It guarantees that mistakes will never happen again
It removes the need to attend training courses

questions.question: Within your agreed ways of working, what does a procedure provide?
options: A record of the training you have completed
The reasons your organisation exists
Your objectives for the year ahead
The step-by-step way to carry out something safely

questions.question: In England, which organisation helps point employers and workers towards funded development?
options: The Care Certificate
Ofsted
Skills for Care
Your local job centre

questions.question: An appraisal is often linked to which of the following?
options: Your monthly payslip
Your original job interview
Your personal development plan
The organisation's safeguarding policy

questions.question: How is induction best described?
options: A single online course that completes the Care Certificate
An optional extra intended only for experienced workers
A test you must survive at the very start of a role
A supported period of learning where you can ask plenty of questions

questions.question: Which of these is an example of 'learning on the job'?
options: Reading national guidance in the evening
Completing an e-learning module at home
Studying for a diploma qualification
Watching an experienced colleague and practising with support

questions.question: You should never wait for a scheduled supervision meeting to report an urgent safeguarding concern.
options: True
False

questions.question: Your agreed ways of working stay fixed once they are written and never need to be updated.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of the following are benefits of reflecting regularly on your practice? (Select all that apply)
options: You become more aware of how your mood and assumptions affect your work
It guarantees you will never make another mistake
You see your progress and identify what to develop next
You notice what works for each person and do more of it
It removes the need for any feedback from others

questions.question: What might a well-kept portfolio of evidence include? (Select all that apply)
options: Certificates of training and qualifications
A printed copy of every policy in the organisation
Written reflections on your experiences
The full, identifiable personal records of everyone you support
Records of observations of your practice at work

questions.question: Each Care Certificate standard has two parts: knowledge, which is what you understand, and ___, which is what you can actually do in practice.
A PDP is only useful when it is treated as a ___ document, rather than a form filled in once and forgotten.
